claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 3 children:
  - Alex is 3 years old and does not live with you.
  - Blake is 2 years old and lives with you.
  - Charlie is 3 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 3 (under 16)
  - Claimant contributes £26.05/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 2 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Charlie:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 3 (under 16)
  - Someone who lives with the child is already claiming
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you some questions.

First, do you have any children you'd like to claim Child Benefit for? If yes, how many children do you have and what are their ages?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I'd like to claim Child Benefit. I have three children - Alex who is 3, Blake who is 2, and Charlie who is also 3.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! You have three children - Alex (3), Blake (2), and Charlie (3). 

Now I need to ask you a few more questions to assess your eligibility:

Do you live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great! Now, do all three of your children (Alex, Blake, and Charlie) live with you in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, not all of them. Blake lives with me, but Alex and Charlie don't live with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying. So Blake lives with you, but Alex and Charlie don't.

For Alex and Charlie who don't live with you - do you contribute financially towards their upkeep (such as paying for food, clothes, pocket money, or contributing to their accommodation)? If so, how much do you contribute per child, and how often?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text:
Yes, I do contribute. For Alex, I help with things like clothes and other bits they need. I contribute about £26 a week for Alex. 

For Charlie, I'm not able to contribute financially at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Just a couple more questions:

1. Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for any of these three children (Alex, Blake, or Charlie)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Well, for Charlie, their other parent who they live with is already claiming Child Benefit. But for Alex and Blake, no one else is claiming.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. I have enough information now to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for each of your children.

Let me make my assessment:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## Your Child Benefit Eligibility Assessment

Based on the information you've provided, here's the outcome for each of your children:

### ✅ **Alex - ELIGIBLE**
You can claim Child Benefit for Alex. Although Alex doesn't live with you, you are contributing £26 per week towards their upkeep (clothes and other needs). Since this amount meets or exceeds the Child Benefit rate, you are considered responsible for Alex under the rules. No one else is claiming for Alex.

### ✅ **Blake - ELIGIBLE**
You can claim Child Benefit for Blake. Blake lives with you in the UK, which means you are responsible for them, and no one else is claiming.

### ❌ **Charlie - NOT ELIGIBLE**
Unfortunately, you cannot claim Child Benefit for Charlie. This is because:
- Charlie's other parent is already claiming Child Benefit for them
- Only one person can receive Child Benefit for the same child
- You are not currently contributing financially to Charlie's upkeep

**Summary:** You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for **2 out of your 3 children** - Alex and Blake.

If you'd like to proceed with claiming, you can apply for Child Benefit for Alex and Blake. If you have any questions or if your circumstances change in the future, please feel free to ask!

reasonings: Alex is 3 years old (under 16) and does not live with you. However, you are contributing £26 per week towards Alex's upkeep (clothes and other needs). Current Child Benefit rates are approximately £25.60 for the eldest child and £16.95 for additional children. Since you are contributing at least the same amount as the Child Benefit rate (£26 per week exceeds the eldest child rate), you are considered responsible for Alex under the rules. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Therefore, you are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.

Blake is 2 years old (under 16) and lives with you in the UK. Since Blake lives with you, you are responsible for them under the eligibility rules. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Blake. Therefore, you are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Blake.

Charlie is 3 years old (under 16) but does not live with you. You are not contributing financially towards Charlie's upkeep. Additionally, Charlie's other parent (who Charlie lives with) is already claiming Child Benefit for Charlie. Under the rules, only one person can get Child Benefit for a child. Since someone else is already claiming for Charlie, you are not eligible to claim Child Benefit for Charlie.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK with three children aged 2-3. Blake lives with them, making them automatically eligible for that child. For Alex (who doesn't live with them), they contribute £26 weekly which meets the threshold for being considered responsible. However, for Charlie, another person is already claiming Child Benefit and the claimant makes no financial contributions, so they cannot claim. Overall: eligible for 2 out of 3 children (Alex and Blake).

Additionally, Charlie's other parent (who Charlie lives with) is already claiming Child Benefit for Charlie. Under the rules, only one person can get Child Benefit for a child. Since someone else is already claiming for Charlie, you are not eligible to claim Child Benefit for Charlie.

For Alex (who doesn't live with them), they contribute £26 weekly which meets the threshold for being considered responsible. However, for Charlie, another person is already claiming Child Benefit and the claimant makes no financial contributions, so they cannot claim.
